Annual Exam - 2020

Code: 65/5/2 (set-2)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  2. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 36 questions.
  4. Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answerbook during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper comprises four Sections A, B, C and D.
    This question paper carries 36 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A - Questions no. 1 to 20 comprises of 20 questions of one mark each.
  3. Section B - Questions no. 21 to 26 comprises of 6 questions of two marks each.
  4. Section C - Questions no. 27 to 32 comprises of 6 questions of four marks each.
  5. Section D - Questions no. 33 to 36 comprises of 4 questions of six marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in 3 questions of one mark, 2 questions of two marks, 2 questions of four marks and 2 questions of six marks. Only one of the choices in such questions have to be attempted.
  7. In addition to this, separate instructions are given with each section and question, wherever necessary.
  8. Use of calculators is not permitted.

Section – A

Question numbers 1 to 10 are multiple choice questions. Select the correct option:

1.

If [x 1]{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = 0, then x equals

a. 0
b. -2
c. -1
d. 2
2.

{tex}\int {/tex} 4x3x dx equals

a. {tex}\frac{12^\text x}{\log 12}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{4^\text x}{\log 4}+\text C{/tex}
c. {tex}\left(\frac{4^\text x \cdot 3^\text x}{\log 4 \cdot \log 3}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{3^\text x}{\log 3}+\text C{/tex}
3.

A number is chosen randomly from numbers 1 to 60. The probability that the chosen number is a multiple of 2 or 5 is

a. {tex}\frac 25{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac 35{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac {7}{10}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac {9}{10}{/tex}
4.

ABCD is a rhombus whose diagonals intersect at E. Then {tex}\vec{\mathrm{EA}}+\vec{\mathrm{EB}}+\vec{\mathrm{EC}}+ \vec{\mathrm{ED}}{/tex} equals

a. {tex}\vec 0{/tex}
b. {tex}\vec {\text {AD}}{/tex}
c. {tex}2\vec {\text {BC}}{/tex}
d. {tex}2\vec {\text {AD}}{/tex}
5.

If A is a square matrix of order 3, such that A (adj A) = 10 I, then |adj A| is equal to:

a. 1
b. 10
c. 100
d. 101
6.

A card is picked at random from a pack of 52 playing cards. Given that the picked card is a queen, the probability of this card to be a card of spade is

a. {tex}\frac{1}{ 3}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{4}{13}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
7.

If {tex}\hat{\text i}{/tex}, {tex}\hat {\text j}{/tex}, {tex}\hat {\text k}{/tex} are unit vectors along three mutually perpendicular directions, then

a. {tex}\hat{\mathbf{i}} \cdot \hat{\mathbf{j}}{/tex} = 1
b. {tex}\hat{\text i} \times \hat{\text j}{/tex} = 1
c. {tex}\hat{\text i} \cdot \hat{\text k}{/tex} = 0
d. {tex}\hat{\text i} \times \hat{\text k}{/tex} = 0
8.

The graph of the inequality 2x + 3y > 6 is

a. half plane that contains the origin.
b. half plane that neither contains the origin nor the points of the line 2x + 3y = 6.
c. whole XOY – plane excluding the points on the line 2x + 3y = 6.
d. entire XOY plane.
9.

The lines {tex}\frac{x-2}{1}=\frac{y-3}{1}=\frac{4-z}{k}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-1}{k}=\frac{y-4}{2}=\frac{z-5}{-2}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular if the value of k is:

a. {tex}-\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
c. -2
d. 2
10.

If y = Ae5x + Be-5x, then {tex}\frac{\text d^{2} \text y}{\text {dx}^{2}}{/tex} is equal to

a. 25 y
b. 5 y
c. -25 y
d. 15 y

Fill in the blanks in Questions from 11 to 15.

11.

A relation R on a set A is called ________, if (a1, a2) {tex}\in{/tex} R and (a2, a3) {tex}\in{/tex} R implies that (a1, a3) {tex}\in{/tex} R, for a1, a2, a3 {tex}\in{/tex} A.
12.

The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+2 \mathrm{y}=x^{2}{/tex} is ________.

OR

The degree of the differential equation {tex}1+\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2{/tex} = x is ________.
13.

The vector equation of the line through the points (3, 4, -7) and (1, -1, 6) is ________.

OR

The line of shortest distance between two skew lines is ________ to both the lines.
14.

If A + B = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} and A - 2B = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} -1 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex}, then A = ________.
15.

The least value of the function f(x) = {tex}ax + \frac{b}{x}{/tex} (a > 0, b > 0, x > 0) is ________.

Q. Nos. 16 to 20 are of very short answer type questions.

16.

Evaluate: {tex}\sin \left[\frac{\pi}{3}-\sin ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)\right]{/tex}.
17.

Using differential, find the approximate value of {tex}\sqrt{36.6}{/tex} upto 2 decimal places.

OR

Find the slope of tangent to the curve y = 2 cos2 (3x) at x = {tex}\frac \pi 6{/tex}.
18.

Find the value of {tex}\int\limits_1^4{/tex} |x - 5| dx.
19.

If the function f defined as f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cc} \frac{\text x^{2}-9}{\text x-3} & , \quad \text x \neq 3 \\ \text k & , \quad \text x=3 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is continuous at x = 3, find the value of k.
20.

For A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} write A-1.

Section – B

Q. Nos. 21 to 26 carry 2 marks each.

21.

Find {tex}\int \frac{\text x+1}{\text x(1-2\text x)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}.
22.

Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{x \sin ^{-1}\left(x^2\right)}{\sqrt{1-x^4}} d x{/tex}
23.

Find the value of {tex}\int\limits_0^1{/tex} x(1 - x)n dx.
24.

If x = a cos {tex}\theta{/tex}; y = b sin {tex}\theta{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{\text d^{2} \text y}{\text {dx}^{2}}{/tex}.

OR

Find the differential sin2 x  w.r.t  ecosx.
25.

Given two independent events A and B such that P(A) = 0.3 and P(B) = 0.6, find {tex}P\left(A^{\prime} \cap B^{\prime}\right){/tex}.
26.

If {tex}\text f(\text x) = \frac{{(4\text x + 3)}}{{(6\text x - 4)}},\;\text x \ne \frac{2}{3}{/tex}, then show that fof(x) = x, for all {tex}\text x \ne \frac{2}{3}{/tex}. Also, write inverse of f.

OR

Check if the relation R in the set R of real numbers defined as R = {tex}\{(\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}): \mathrm{a}<\mathrm{b}\}{/tex} is

  1. symmetric
  2. transitive

Section – C

Q. Nos. 27 to 32 carry 4 marks each.

27.

Prove that tan{tex}\left[2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\cot ^{-1} 3\right]=\frac{9}{13}{/tex}.
28.

If y = (cos x)x + tan-1 {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex}, find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}.
29.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex} represent two adjacent sides of a parallelogram, find unit vectors parallel to the diagonals of the parallelogram.

OR

Using vectors, find the area of the triangle ABC with vertices A(1, 2, 3), B(2, -1, 4) and C(4, 5, -1).
30.

A company manufactures two types of novelty souvenirs made of plywood. Souvenirs of type A requires 5 minutes each for cutting and 10 minutes each for assembling. Souvenirs of type B require 8 minutes each for cutting and 8 minutes each for assembling. Given that total time for cutting is 3 hours 20 minutes and for assembling 4 hours. The profit for type A souvenir ₹ 100 each and for type B souvenir, profit is ₹120 each. How many souvenirs of each type should the company manufacture in order to maximize the profit? Formulate the problem as an LPP and solve it graphically.
31.

Three rotten apples are mixed with seven fresh apples. Find the probability distribution of the number of rotten apples, if three apples are drawn one by one with replacement. Find the mean of the number of rotten apples.

OR

In a shop X, 30 tins of ghee of type A and 40 tins of ghee of type B which look alike, are kept for sale. While in shop Y, similar 50 tins of ghee of type A and 60 tins of ghee of type B are there. One tin of ghee is purchased from one of the randomly selected shop and is found to be of type B. Find the probability that it is purchased from shop Y.
32.

Solve the differential equation:
{tex}x \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x-\mathrm{y} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right)=0{/tex}
Given that x = 1 when y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

Section – D

Q. 33 to 36, carry 6 marks each.

33.

If a, b, c are pth, qth and rth terms respectively of a G.P, then prove that {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} {\log a} & {p} & {1} \\ {\log b} & {q} & {1} \\ {\log c} & {r} & {1} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0

OR

If A ={tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, then find A-1.
Using A-1, solve the following system of equations:
2x - 3y + 5z = 11
3x + 2y - 4z = -5
x + y - 2z = -3
34.

Find the vector and cartesian equations of the line which is perpendicular to the lines with equations {tex}\frac{x+2}{1}=\frac{y-3}{2}=\frac{z+1}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and passes through the point (1, 1, 1). Also find the angle between the given lines.
35.

Using integration find the area of the region bounded between the two circles x2 + y2 = 9 and (x - 3)2 + y2 = 9.

OR

Evaluate the following integral as the limit of sums {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{4}\left(\text x^{2}-\text x\right) {\text {dx}}{/tex}.
36.

Find the point on the curve y2 = 4x which is nearest to the point (2,1).

Correct Answer: (b) {tex}\frac 35{/tex}
Explanation:

We need to find the probability that a randomly chosen number from 1 to 60 is a multiple of 2 or 5.

  1. Multiples of 2 : Numbers divisible by 2 in the range 1 to 60 are: 2, 4, 6, ..., 60. There are 30 such numbers.
  2. Multiples of 5 : Numbers divisible by 5 in the range 1 to 60 are: 5, 10, 15, ..., 60. There are 12 such numbers.
  3. Multiples of both 2 and 5 (i.e., multiples of 10): Numbers divisible by 10 in the range 1 to 60 are: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. There are 6 such numbers.

Using the principle of inclusion-exclusion, the total number of favorable outcomes is:
Multiples of 2 or 5 = 30 + 12 - 6 = 36
Thus, the probability is:
{tex} P=\frac{36}{60}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

    Three rotten apples are mixed with seven fresh apples. Find the probability distribution of the number of rotten apples, if three apples are drawn one by one with replacement. Find the mean of the number of rotten apples.

    Solution:

    Let X be a random variable denoting the number of rotten apples.Then,
    X = 0, 1, 2, 3
    P(0) = no rotten apple is drawn
    P(0) = {tex}\frac{^{7} C_{3}}{^{10} C_{3}}=\frac{7 \times 6 \times 5}{10 \times 9 \times 8}=\frac{7}{24}{/tex}
    P(1) = one rotten apple and two fresh apples are drawn
    P(1) = {tex}\frac{^{3} C_{1} \times^{7} C_{2}}{^{10} C_{3}}=\frac{3 \times 3 \times 7}{5 \times 3 \times 8}=\frac{21}{40}{/tex}
    P(2) = two rotten apples and one fresh apple are drawn
    P(2) = {tex}\frac{^{3} C_{2} \times^{7} C_{1}}{^{10} C_{3}}=\frac{3 \times 7}{5 \times 3 \times 8}=\frac{7}{40}{/tex}
    P(3) = three rotten apples are drawn
    P(3) = {tex}\frac{^{3} C_{3}}{^{10} C_{3}}=\frac{1}{120}{/tex}

    Therefore, the probability distribution of the discrete random variable X is

    X 0 1 2 3
    P(X) {tex}7\over 24{/tex} {tex}21 \over 40{/tex} {tex}7 \over 40{/tex} {tex}1 \over 120{/tex}

    The mean of the distribution,
    {tex}\mu=\sum_{x=0}^{3} X \times P(X){/tex}
    {tex}\mu{/tex} = {tex}0 \times \frac{7}{24}{/tex} + {tex}1 \times \frac{21}{40}{/tex} + {tex}2 \times \frac{7}{40}{/tex} + {tex}3 \times \frac{1}{120}{/tex}
    {tex}\mu{/tex} = {tex}\frac{36}{40}=\frac{9}{10}{/tex}

    Given that x = 1 when y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

    Solution:

    {tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x-y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0 .{/tex}


    {tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x {/tex}
    {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x}{x\left(\sin \frac{y}{x}\right)} {/tex}


    Now {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
    nut {tex}x=\lambda x, y=\lambda y{/tex}

    {tex} f(\lambda x, \lambda y) =\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}\right) {/tex}
    {tex} =\lambda^0\left[\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] {/tex}

    Now put, {tex}y=v x{/tex} ins eq n (1) get, and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}. we get.

    {tex} +v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{x(v \sin v-1)}{x \sin v} {/tex}
    {tex} x+x \frac{d v}{d x} =v-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex}

    {tex} -\int \sin v d v=\int \frac{d x}{x} {/tex}
    {tex} \cos v=\log |x|+C {/tex}

    {tex} 0=0+c {/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow c=0 {/tex}

    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Particular Sol {tex}{ }^{\circ}{/tex} is

    {tex} \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|{/tex}

    Solution:

    Let A be the first term and R be the common ratio of the G.P. Then,
    a = ARP-1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log a = log A + (p - 1) log R
    b = ARq-1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} logb = log A + (q -1 ) log R
    c = ARr-1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log c = log A + (r - 1) log R
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {\log A+(p-1)log R} & {p} & {1} \\ {\log A+(q-1)} log R & {q} & {1} \\ {\log A+(r-1) \log R} & {r} & {1} \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {\log A+(p-1) \log R} & {p-1} & {1} \\ {\log A+(q-1) \log R} & {q-1} & {1} \\ {\log A+(r-1) \log R} & {r-1} & {1} \end{array}\right|{/tex} [By applying C2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} C2 - C3]
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {0} & {p-1} & {1} \\ {0} & {q-1} & {1} \\ {0} & {r-1} & {1} \end{array}\right|{/tex} [By applying C1 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} C1 - (log A) C3 - (log R) C2]
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 0

    Find the point on the curve y2 = 4x which is nearest to the point (2,1).

    Solution:

    Suppose (x, y) be a point on y2 = 4x and A(2,1) be the given point. Then,
    AP2 = (x -2)2 + (y - 1)2
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AP2 = {tex}\left(\frac{y^{2}}{4}-2\right)^{2}{/tex} + (y - 1)2 [ {tex}\because{/tex} y2 = 4x {tex}\because{/tex} x = y2 / 4]
    Suppose  Z = Ap2. Then, Z is maximum or minimum according as AP is maximum or minimum.
    Now, Z= {tex}\left(\frac{y^{2}}{4}-2\right)^{2}{/tex} + (y - 2)2
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d Z}{d y}{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\frac{y^{2}}{4}-2\right)\left(\frac{2 y}{4}\right){/tex} + 2(y -1) = {tex}\frac{y^{3}}{4}{/tex} - 2 and, {tex}\frac{d^{2} Z}{d y^{2}}=\frac{3 y^{2}}{4}{/tex}.
    The critical points of Z are given by {tex}\frac{d Z}{d y}{/tex} = 0.
    {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{d Z}{d y}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{y^{3}}{4}{/tex} - 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y3 = 8 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2
    Clearly, {tex}\left(\frac{d^{2} \mathrm{Z}}{d y^{2}}\right)_{y=2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3(2)^{2}}{4}{/tex} = 3 > 0. Therefore, z is minimum when y = 2.
    Putting y = 2 in y2 = 4x, we obtain x = 1. Thus , the coordinates of P are (1,2).
    Thus, the point (1,2) on y2 = 4x is nearest to the point (2,1).
